meta.title: scry ingest wire protocol
meta.description: Agent ↔ server wire protocol for shipping metrics, logs, traces, and profiles to a scry ingest server. Long-lived TCP stream, length-framed messages, batch payloads compressed independently. Designed to be the only thing agents speak: the server's HTTP query surface is separate.

types.//1: Framing. Every message on the wire is `[len: u32][Frame]` where
types.//2: Frame's first byte is the message-type discriminator. The length
types.//3: covers Frame, not the length prefix itself.

Frame.description: Top-level discriminated union of all messages. Peek-discriminated on the message-type byte; each variant struct begins with a const-tagged uint8 that matches the discriminator.

Hello.description: First message from agent. Identifies agent, advertises capabilities and the signals it intends to send. Sent exactly once at connection open.

sequence.name: protocol_version
sequence.//: Major.minor packed: (major << 8) | minor. v0 = 0x0001.

sequence.name: agent_id
sequence.//: UUID v7 of the agent process; stable across reconnects, regenerated on reinstall.

sequence.name: agent_version
sequence.//: Semver build of the scry-agent binary, e.g. '0.2.1-abc1234'.

sequence.name: signals
sequence.//: Bitmask: 0x01=metrics, 0x02=logs, 0x04=traces, 0x08=profiles. Agent only sends batches for signals it announces here.

sequence.name: capabilities
sequence.//: Bitmask of optional features the agent supports. See protocol.constants.CAP_*.

sequence.name: resource_attrs
sequence.//: OTel-style resource attributes that apply to every record from this agent unless overridden (host, service, env, region, …).

HelloAck.description: Server's response to Hello. Confirms protocol version, identifies the server's writer_id (D-015), and gives the agent its session_id for the lifetime of this connection.

sequence.name: protocol_version
sequence.//: Negotiated version. Server picks min(agent.version, server.max_supported). Agent disconnects if unsupported.

sequence.name: writer_id
sequence.//: Server's stable writer_id. Goes into block paths and ack receipts so the agent can reason about which server holds which data.

sequence.name: session_id
sequence.//: Opaque, server-assigned. Echoed in every Batch and every Ack for this connection so logs are correlatable.

sequence.name: capabilities
sequence.//: Server's feature bitmask. Agent must not use a capability not present in this AND its own.

sequence.name: suggested_batch_bytes
sequence.//: Soft hint: agent should aim for batches around this size (after compression) for best throughput. Default 4 MiB.

sequence.name: max_batch_bytes
sequence.//: Hard limit: server will reject batches exceeding this size. Default 16 MiB.

sequence.name: max_inflight_batches
sequence.//: Server-side per-connection inflight cap. Agent must not have more than this number of un-acked batches outstanding.

types.//9: Batches. The bulk of the protocol by byte count.

Batch.description: A compressed, signal-specific batch of records. The payload, after decompression, is a SignalBatch variant. Each batch carries its own compression: a connection may interleave compressed and uncompressed batches if the agent decides per-batch.

sequence.name: session_id
sequence.//: Must match HelloAck.session_id. A mismatch is a hard protocol error.

sequence.name: batch_id
sequence.//: Monotonic per agent across the lifetime of the connection. Used by the agent to correlate BatchAcks.

sequence.name: signal
sequence.//: 1=metrics, 2=logs, 3=traces, 4=profiles. Must be one of the bits announced in Hello.signals.

sequence.name: ts_min_unix_nano
sequence.//: Earliest timestamp in the batch. Lets the server pick the right (signal, day) block builder without inspecting the payload.

sequence.name: ts_max_unix_nano
sequence.//: Latest timestamp in the batch.

sequence.name: record_count
sequence.//: Number of records inside the (decompressed) payload. Lets the server reject suspicious batches before decompressing.

sequence.name: compression
sequence.//: 0=none, 1=zstd. zstd is the only compression we'll bother shipping in v1; future codecs go here.

sequence.name: uncompressed_size
sequence.//: Decompressed payload size in bytes. Lets the server pre-size buffers and refuse to allocate if it would blow ingest_buffers budget (see Resource isolation).

sequence.name: payload
sequence.//: Compressed bytes of a SignalBatch variant. After decompression, decode according to `signal`: 1=MetricsBatch, 2=LogsBatch, 3=TracesBatch, 4=ProfilesBatch.

BatchAck.description: Server's response per Batch. Either accepted (durable in WAL), throttled (agent should slow down before retrying), or rejected (do not retry — fix or drop).

sequence.name: batch_id
sequence.//: Echoed from the Batch this acks.

sequence.name: status
sequence.//: 0=accepted, 1=throttled, 2=rejected.

sequence.name: retry_after_ms
sequence.//: When status=throttled: agent should wait at least this long before retrying. When accepted/rejected: 0.

sequence.name: reason_code
sequence.//: When status=rejected: a stable numeric reason (see protocol.constants.REJECT_*). 0 otherwise.

sequence.name: message
sequence.//: Optional human-readable detail. Empty on accept. Used for operator-visible logs, not for programmatic decisions.

types.//12: Out-of-band flow control. Server can push an asynchronous rate
types.//13: hint at any time (e.g. when a signal's token bucket is draining,
types.//14: or when the dynamic compaction throttle pauses).

FlowControl.description: Asynchronous server→agent rate hint. Not a response to any specific Batch. Per-signal so the server can throttle (say) traces without affecting metrics.

sequence.name: signal
sequence.//: Signal this hint applies to; 0xFF = all signals.

sequence.name: max_bytes_per_sec
sequence.//: Suggested sustained ceiling. 0 = clear any prior throttle.

sequence.name: max_batches_inflight
sequence.//: Suggested per-signal inflight cap, overrides HelloAck.max_inflight_batches for this signal. 0 = no signal-specific cap.

sequence.name: valid_for_ms
sequence.//: Hint's validity window. After this elapses with no fresh FlowControl, agent reverts to defaults.

Ping.description: Either side may send. Receiver MUST reply with a Pong carrying the same nonce.

Goodbye.description: Graceful close. Either side may send. After sending Goodbye, the sender must not send any new Batch; it may still send/receive BatchAcks for already-inflight batches.

sequence.name: reason_code
sequence.//: See GOODBYE_* constants. 0 = normal shutdown.

Error.description: Protocol-level error. Receiving an Error MUST close the connection. Distinct from BatchAck.rejected, which is a per-batch decision the connection survives.

sequence.name: code
sequence.//: See protocol.constants.ERR_*.

types.//23: Payload types. These are the *decompressed* contents of

LabelPair.description: A key-value label. Used by every signal. UTF-8 throughout.

types.//27: Metrics. Series-dictionary compression: full labels appear once
types.//28: per (series_fingerprint) per batch, samples reference fingerprint.

MetricsBatch.description: Body of a metrics Batch after decompression.

sequence.name: series
sequence.//: Dictionary entries for series first introduced in this batch. Agents maintain a per-session seen-set; servers reject Sample references to fingerprints never declared.

SeriesDictEntry.description: Declares a series (set of labels) and its xxh3-64 fingerprint. The agent computes the fingerprint; the server validates it on receipt and rejects mismatches.

sequence.name: fingerprint
sequence.//: xxh3-64 of canonically-sorted labels. Stable across agents.

sequence.name: metric_type
sequence.//: 0=unknown, 1=counter, 2=gauge, 3=histogram, 4=summary. Cheap hint, not authoritative — the query engine treats series as untyped.

MetricSample.description: One scalar observation. Histograms and summaries explode into multiple samples (one per bucket / one per quantile) before serialisation; the wire knows only scalars.

sequence.name: fingerprint
sequence.//: Reference to a SeriesDictEntry from this batch or any earlier batch in this session.

sequence.name: streams
sequence.//: Records are pre-grouped by (label set) at the agent so labels aren't repeated per line. Within a stream, entries share the same labels.

sequence.name: fingerprint
sequence.//: Same fingerprint discipline as metrics (xxh3-64 of sorted labels). Lets the server pre-bucket by stream cheaply.

sequence.name: severity
sequence.//: OTel SeverityNumber: 0=unspecified, 1-4=trace, 5-8=debug, 9-12=info, 13-16=warn, 17-20=error, 21-24=fatal.

sequence.name: body
sequence.//: uint32 length because individual log lines can legitimately exceed 64KB (stack traces, JSON dumps).

sequence.name: attributes
sequence.//: Per-entry attributes that aren't part of the stream's stable label set (request id, user id, …). Indexed but not used for stream partitioning.

types.//34: Traces. OTel-aligned but stripped of the wire-format pain points

sequence.name: resources
sequence.//: Per-batch resource dictionary. Spans reference an index, not the labels directly.

sequence.name: resource_idx
sequence.//: Index into TracesBatch.resources.

sequence.name: scope_idx
sequence.//: Index into TracesBatch.scopes.

sequence.name: parent_span_id
sequence.//: Absent for root spans.

sequence.name: kind
sequence.//: OTel SpanKind: 0=unspecified, 1=internal, 2=server, 3=client, 4=producer, 5=consumer.

sequence.name: status_code
sequence.//: OTel StatusCode: 0=unset, 1=ok, 2=error.

types.//38: Profiles. v0.1: opaque pprof bytes per sample-set; structured
types.//39: schema lives in `## Open questions` of ARCHITECTURE.md and will
types.//40: land in v0.4. We keep the framing so the wire doesn't change

sequence.name: ts_unix_nano
sequence.//: Wall-clock when the profile window ended.

sequence.name: duration_nano
sequence.//: Profiling window length.

sequence.name: labels
sequence.//: Identifies the profiled subject: service, instance, profile type (cpu/heap/goroutine/…).

sequence.name: format
sequence.//: 1=pprof_gz (gzip-compressed pprof). Other formats reserved.

sequence.name: data
sequence.//: The opaque blob. Server stores it as-is in v0.1; v0.4 will denormalise on ingest.

protocol.name: scry-ingest
protocol.description: Agent → server ingest protocol. Long-lived TCP stream, length-framed messages (`[len: u32][Frame]`), per-batch zstd compression on the payload only.
notes: Framing: every message on the wire is preceded by a `uint32` length covering the Frame bytes (not the length itself).
Compression: applied per Batch.payload only. Frame, headers, and acks are always uncompressed so observers can read framing without zstd state.
Endianness: big-endian throughout. We accept the trivial cost for the readability/debuggability win.
Ordering: BatchAcks are not required to be in batch_id order. Agents must correlate by batch_id, not by arrival order.
Concurrency: at most HelloAck.max_inflight_batches Batch messages without their ack. Agent must apply backpressure to its collectors when this cap is reached.
Fingerprints: xxh3-64 over canonically-sorted (UTF-8 NFC) label pairs encoded as `key\0value\0` repeated. Server validates on receipt. Agents that send mismatched fingerprints get a hard Error.

Versioning: a server that does not support the agent's announced protocol_version replies with an Error(code=ERR_PROTOCOL_VERSION) and closes. Agents fall back to a lower advertised version, not silently retry.

messages.name: HelloAck
messages.description: Confirm session, return server identity and per-connection limits.

messages.name: Batch
messages.description: Compressed batch of records for one signal.

messages.name: Error
messages.description: Protocol-level error; receipt closes the connection.

ACK_ACCEPTED.description: Batch is durable in the WAL.

ACK_THROTTLED.description: Server is overloaded; retry after retry_after_ms.

ACK_REJECTED.description: Permanent failure; do not retry. See reason_code.

REJECT_BAD_SCHEMA.description: Payload could not be decoded against the announced signal's schema.

REJECT_BAD_FINGERPRINT.description: A SeriesDictEntry.fingerprint did not match its labels.

REJECT_UNKNOWN_SERIES.description: MetricSample referenced a fingerprint never declared this session.

REJECT_TIMESTAMP_OUT_OF_RANGE.description: Records older than retention or further into the future than the server's clock-skew budget.

REJECT_CARDINALITY_LIMIT.description: Series introduction would breach a per-tenant cardinality cap (if configured).

REJECT_INTERNAL.description: Server-side bug or transient internal failure; agent may retry after a backoff.

ERR_BAD_FRAMING.description: Length prefix or message tag is malformed.

ERR_DUPLICATE_HELLO.description: Second Hello on the same connection.

ERR_SESSION_MISMATCH.description: Batch carried a session_id other than this connection's.

ERR_AUTH.description: Authentication / authorization failure (reserved; auth model is an open question).

ERR_INTERNAL.description: Unexpected server-side error; connection is closed.

GOODBYE_SERVER_DRAINING.description: Server is shutting down; agent should reconnect to a different server (re-resolve via discovery).

GOODBYE_AGENT_RELOAD.description: Agent is reloading config and will reconnect.

CAP_RESERVED.description: No capabilities defined in v0.1; bitmask reserved for future use.
